skill.name: monorepo-management
skill.description: Guides setup and operation of JavaScript and TypeScript monorepos using Turborepo, Nx, pnpm workspaces, shared packages, caching, CI, and release workflows.
skill.summary: Build and maintain scalable monorepos with Turborepo, Nx, pnpm, shared configs, CI, and package publishing.
skill.author: wshobson

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged command execution, hidden file access, token references, a hardcoded URL, and heuristic combinations. Review found SKILL.md is documentation with fenced examples for Turborepo, Nx, pnpm, CI, and releases; no prompt injection, obfuscated code, secret exfiltration, or executable skill scripts were found. Low risk remains because users may copy commands that install packages, remove node_modules, deploy, or publish packages.

low_findings.title: Package and Shell Commands Are Documentation Examples
low_findings.description: The repeated external command matches occur inside fenced examples for creating workspaces, running pnpm and nx tasks, CI jobs, deployment, and publishing. This is not active code execution by the skill, but copied commands can modify repositories, install packages, deploy apps, or publish packages.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line ranges are fenced documentation snippets, not executable files or skill scripts. The commands are legitimate monorepo operations, but they have real effects if a user copies them into a shell.

low_findings.title: Hidden File and Environment File References Are Configuration Examples
low_findings.description: The hidden file findings point to sample configuration paths such as .env patterns, .npmrc, .eslintrc.js, and .github workflows. No evidence found that the skill reads, writes, or exfiltrates these files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The references are visible file names in configuration examples. There is no code path that opens local hidden files or collects environment file contents.

low_findings.title: CI Secret Names Appear in a Release Workflow Example
low_findings.description: GITHUB_TOKEN and NPM_TOKEN are shown as GitHub Actions secret names for a package release example. This is normal CI documentation and does not send secrets to an untrusted endpoint in the skill content.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The token names appear only inside a release workflow snippet using changesets/action. The context supports legitimate publishing, though users should keep token scopes minimal.

low_findings.title: Static Heuristic Combination Dismissed After Context Review
low_findings.description: The critical combination of command, network, filesystem, and credential signals is caused by unrelated documentation examples. The hardcoded URL is a schema reference, line 3 does not contain cryptography, and the dependency graph text is not system reconnaissance.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review confirmed these static matches are documentation artifacts rather than a coherent malicious behavior chain. No prompt injection language or hidden execution mechanism was found.

content.user_title: Manage Monorepos with Turborepo, Nx, and pnpm
content.value_statement: Monorepos can become slow and inconsistent when packages, builds, and releases are not coordinated. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code practical patterns for workspace setup, caching, CI, and shared tooling.
actual_capabilities: Explains when to use monorepos and the tradeoffs of shared repositories.
Provides starter structures for Turborepo, Nx, and pnpm workspace projects.
Shows examples for shared UI packages, utilities, types, ESLint, and TypeScript configs.
Guides build optimization with dependency graphs, affected tasks, and cache outputs.
Includes CI examples for install, build, test, lint, type check, deploy, and release steps.
Covers package publishing with Changesets and GitHub Actions secret references.
limitations: The skill is documentation only and does not inspect a repository automatically.
Version examples may need updates for current Turborepo, Nx, pnpm, and GitHub Actions releases.
It focuses on JavaScript and TypeScript monorepos, not polyglot enterprise build systems.
Security, access control, and package publishing choices still need project-specific review.

use_cases.title: Set Up a New Product Monorepo
use_cases.description: Create a workspace layout with apps, packages, shared configs, and repeatable scripts.

use_cases.title: Improve Slow Builds and Tests
use_cases.description: Tune task dependencies, cache outputs, affected project commands, and parallel execution.

use_cases.title: Standardize Shared Packages
use_cases.description: Define UI, utility, type, ESLint, and TypeScript package patterns across applications.

prompt_templates.title: Choose a Monorepo Tool
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me choose between Turborepo, Nx, and pnpm workspaces for a TypeScript monorepo with two apps and shared packages.

prompt_templates.title: Design the Workspace Layout
prompt_templates.prompt: Propose a monorepo folder structure, root scripts, package naming scheme, and shared configuration packages for our product workspace.

prompt_templates.title: Optimize Build Performance
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this monorepo build plan and recommend task dependencies, cache outputs, affected commands, and parallel execution settings.

prompt_templates.title: Plan Releases and CI
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a release and CI strategy for a pnpm monorepo using Changesets, GitHub Actions, scoped package publishing, and protected secrets.

output_examples.input: Plan a Turborepo layout for a web app, docs app, shared UI package, and shared TypeScript config.
output: Recommended workspace folders for apps and packages.
Root scripts for build, dev, test, lint, and type checking.
Shared package boundaries and dependency rules.

output_examples.input: Our monorepo CI is slow. Suggest practical improvements.
output: A list of cache inputs and outputs to verify.
Affected project commands for pull requests.
Parallel task settings and dependency graph checks.

output_examples.input: We need to publish internal packages from a monorepo.
output: Guidance for token scope and CI secret handling.
Checks before publishing public or private packages.

best_practices: Keep package boundaries clear and avoid circular dependencies between workspaces.
Define cache inputs and outputs explicitly for build, test, lint, and type-check tasks.
Use least-privilege CI tokens and protect package publishing workflows with review controls.
anti_patterns: Do not copy publish or deploy commands into production workflows without reviewing token scope.
Do not rely on phantom dependencies that are not declared in each package manifest.
Do not share every utility by default when local ownership would keep packages simpler.

faq.question: Does this skill create a monorepo automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides guidance and examples that an AI assistant can adapt to your repository.

faq.question: Which monorepo tools does it cover?
faq.answer: It covers Turborepo, Nx, pnpm workspaces, npm workspaces, Yarn workspaces, Lerna, and Changesets.

faq.question: Is it only for JavaScript projects?
faq.answer: The examples are mainly JavaScript and TypeScript focused, especially Node.js package workspaces.

faq.question: Can it help with CI performance?
faq.answer: Yes. It explains affected tasks, caching, parallel execution, and GitHub Actions patterns.

faq.question: Does it include security guidance?
faq.answer: It includes CI token examples, but teams should still review secret scope and publishing permissions.

faq.question: Can Claude Code or Codex use this in an existing repository?
faq.answer: Yes. They can use the guidance to inspect structure, suggest changes, and implement repository-specific updates.
